Dear Ms. Turvill:
In response to your letter dated August 76,7990, (see enclosed copy) I submit the following
answers to three questions raised in regard to the Conflict of Interest Code for the Center
for Mzrnagement Development:
L h,MT' Associates, Inc. was incorporated in the State of California in 1981.
2. EMT Associates, [nc. was not created as a result of any contractual relationship with
the Center for Management Development'
3. When EMT contracts with trainers for specific training workshops, these are
exclusively available to the members and affiliates of the Center for Management
Development. We develop a new roster of courses and trainers from year to year,
under new contracts, based on needs expressed by the Center for Management
Development.
Thank you for your inquiry. If I can provide additional information please contact me
directly.
Sincerely,

DESCRIPTION OF SERVICES PROVIDED BY EMT ASSOCIATES. INC..
UNDER CONTRACT TO THE CENTER FOR MANAGEMENT DEVELOPMENT
By virtue of the authority vested in a Joint Powers Agreement (among the cities of Davis,
Fairfield, Napa, Vacaville, Woodland, Benicia, L-odi Public Works Department and the
counties of San Joaquin and Solano), the Center for Management Development contracts
with EMT Associates, Inc., to provide manaqement training workshops for public e mplovees.
EMT Associates has been providing this service under annual contracts since 1982.
The services rendered by EMT Associates include conducting a training needs assessment
surv-eJ, a!.the close of, each-contragl yp-ar-to--,id!_rl!ifu gqurs-es--o-tlltl.e_UleI9$L_Egllgyl4g-
the Seeds qssglsqlenl, we propose a roster an schedule of courses and a budget estimate of
costs to render these services. Specific tasks associated with this contract include:
J.
Distribute, collect, and analyze annual training needs assessment survey data;
Prepare a roster of 30-35 courses to be offered between September and Mav;
Contact communiw facilities and trainer consultants to schedule courses. and contract
with trainers:
4. Disseminate descriptions of courses, schedule, and registration forms;
5. Log all registrations, develop roll sheets, and monitor enrollment from each
participating jurisdiction (based on number of training slots purchases in advance);
6. For each course, EMT staff prepare workshop handouts, arrange for use of audio-
visual equipment, and fulfill all logistical requirements in advance and on site at the
training location;
7. For each course, EMT staff return to training site to 'break down" the set up, return
equipment, and collect evaluation summaries from course participants.
8. Submit an invoice and monthly report to CMD members and affiliates, describing
registration and attendance, and evaluation for the month.
9. Attend meetings with CMD members and affiliates as needed, usually three or four
times per contract period.
10. At the close of each year, EMT provides a cumulative summary of all registration an
attendance data, by jurisdiction, as well as final course evaluation scores.
To carry out this contract EMT relies mostly on our staff receptionist to conduct all logistical
and day to day tasks. A senior professional directs the contract, furnishing the monthly
reports, analyzing the annual needs assessment data, and planning the schedule for the
coming contract year. EMT's contract covers expenses for staff, consultant trainer fees and
expenses, operational costs such as rent, utilities, telephone, photocopfng, personal auto
mileage, supplies, and computer rental.

trte tblitical Reform Act, covernment code Sections 81000, et seq.,
requires state and local goverrment agencies to adopt and pronulgate Conflict
of Interest Codes. the Fair Political Practices Cqrmissi.on has adopted a
regulation, 2 Ca]. A&n. Code Section 18730, which contains the tenILS of a
standard conflict of Interest Code, vihich can be incorporaEed by reference,
- and which may be anended by the Fair Political Practices Comission to clnform

Therefore, the terns of 2 cal. Adn. Code Section 18730 and any amendnents to
it duly adopted by the Fair Political Practic€s Cqnnission are hereby

EMT
EMT Associates, Inc.
Evalualion, Management and Training
3090 Fite Circle. Suite 20'l
Sacramento. CA 95827
(916) 363-9415
Jur il
,, ;1 ,;i 'S
July 11, 1990
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814
RE: Who Must File Statements of Economic Interests
Dear SirMadame:
EMT Associates, Inc., is a management consulting firm in Sacramento. Our clients are
government agencies such as the Department of Alcohol and Drug Programs, the California
Youth Authority, the Arizona Depa ment of Corrections, federal agencies like the National
Institute of Justice, or local agencies such as the City Council of Sacramento. We engage
in competitive bid contracting for our work, or extensions thereof.
Recently the City of Fairfield requested EMT's President, Joel Phillips, complete a Form 730
to comply with reporting requirements stated therein. Upon review of the form and the
language identiffing who must file a Form 730, EMT respectfully requests a ruling from the
Fair Political Practices Commission indicating we are neither the type of entity targeted by
the conflict of interest disclosure nor are we at risk of engaging in such activities.
The City of Fairfield has requested EMT's response with appropriate ruling from the Fair
Political Practices Commission. I enclose a copy of the latest correspondence from Yolanda
Irigon of City of Fairfield, a copy of Fairfield's Conflict of Interest Code, and a description
of EMT's contractual services pertaining to her letter.
As I understand the instructions, Form 730 is for (1) elected officials, or (2) designated
employees who make decisions which affect the financial interests of the state or local
government agenry. We are neither.


Fair Political Practices Commission
July 11, 1990
Page 2
According to the definition of who must file, statements of economic interest:
WHO MUST FILE STATEMENTS OF ECONOMIC INTERESTS?
All state and local government agencies (including cities and counties) are re qtired
to adopt conflict of interest codes designating certain positions which entail the
making or pafticipation in the making of decision which may affect financial interests.
You must file a Statement of Economic Intercst (Form 730) because you arc an
employee or an appointed official whose position is designated in your agency's
conflict of interest code. C-ertain elected officials not specified in Gov. Code Section
87200 file form 730 if re qtired by a conflict of interest code.
As a consultant to the Center for Management Develop, (CMD) EMT Associates is
designated in CMD's Conflict of Interest Code because we provide professional services to
CMD, under category 2 of disclosure categories. However, the specific professional services
are not defined in the code, and the
Chairperson may determine in writing that a particular consultant, although a
"Designated Employee", is hired to perform a range of duties that arc limited in
scope and thus is not re qtired to fully comply with the disclosure re qtirement
descrikd in the section.
I maintain that EMT's duties are indeed, limited in scope and request that the Chairperson
to CMD grant us such an exception. The reasons for this request follow:
1. EMT Associates acts in behalf of CMD as a contractor to broker for training services
among up to 35 individual training consultants.
2. EMT is under contract to CMD for 10 months out of the year, al a level of effort
that is the equivalent of .25 FTE.
3. EMT's duties are narrowly confined to scheduling, logistics, and monthly reporting
4. EMT plays no role in decisions that effect financial interests of CMD or the City of
Fairfield. There is no opportunity for a conflict of interest to arise in the context of
EMT's contract with CMD.


Fair Political Practices Commission
July 11, 1990
Page 3
EMT's reluctance to simply comple te the Form 730 and submit it as requested is a function
of two issues:
1. Once we complete Form 730, are we likely to subject ourselves to similar
requirements from all our California contracting agencie s? We hope to avoid
establishing an unnecessary precedent.
2. The nature of our work and contractual relationships does not appear to fit either
description of who must file. We would be more than willing to submit a Form 730
if we understood the rationale for our casc.
Please review the enclosed documents and direct your written finding to Yolanda Irigon with
the City of Fairfield, 10(X) Webster Street, Fairfield, CA 94533-4883 and send a copy to me
or Jo€l at EMT. You may contact either Jo€l Phillips or me to discuss and resolve this
issue. Thank you in advance for your consideration.
Sincerely,
"ll,t-* 
p. C*"tX
Lynne P. Cannady
LPCha
Enclosures

sections.question_synthetic: EMT Associates, Inc. seeks a ruling from the FPPC clarifying whether its president must file Form 730 (Statement of Economic Interests) when the company contracts with government agencies like the City of Fairfield, arguing that EMT is neither an elected official nor a designated employee making decisions affecting the agency's financial interests.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request letter (primary) plus supporting materials including a conflict of interest code, notice of intent to adopt code, and service descriptions. The primary document is an incoming request from EMT Associates to FPPC, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No FPPC conclusion section found in document.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: EMT Associates, Inc. seeks a ruling from the FPPC clarifying whether its president must file Form 730 (Statement of Economic Interests) when the company contracts with government agencies like the City of Fairfield, arguing that EMT is neither an elected official nor a designated employee making decisions affecting the agency's financial interests.

embedding.summary: This document is an incoming request to the FPPC from EMT Associates, Inc., seeking clarification on whether its president must file statements of economic interests (Form 730) under the City of Fairfield's conflict of interest code. The request includes background on EMT's consulting services, the Center for Management Development's conflict of interest code, and correspondence addressing the applicability of disclosure requirements to EMT as a contractor.
